key_insight: N-RSAV speeds up RSAV optimization by injecting randomized low-rank Hessian curvature while preserving an unconditional modified energy dissipation law.
engineering_relevance: The method is especially useful for ill-conditioned PDE-constrained optimization and PINN training, where first-order RSAV methods converge too slowly.
method: Build a linear operator from a randomized Nyström low-rank Hessian approximation.
Truncate negative eigenvalues to enforce positive semidefiniteness and maintain dissipation.
Reuse the approximate Hessian adaptively based on the gap between original and modified energies.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on ill-conditioned low-rank problems, including convex quadratics and PINN training, showing substantially faster convergence than standard RSAV methods.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and reuse policy of the approximate Hessian, and eigenvalue truncation may discard useful curvature in some problems.
paper_title: Accelerating SAV-based optimization via randomized low-rank Hessian approximation
